seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Cape Burney and Cape Range National Park. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Cape Burney vs Cape Range National Park: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Cape Burney and Cape Range National Park.
heading: Cape Burney vs Cape Range National Park — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Cape Burney, Western Australia and Cape Range National Park, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Cape Range National Park has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Cape Burney (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Cape Burney or Cape Range National Park safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Cape Burney has a flood risk score of 21/100, while Cape Range National Park scores 22/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Cape Burney scores 64/100 for bushfire risk. Cape Range National Park scores 64/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Cape Burney or Cape Range National Park?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Cape Range National Park has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.
